title: Micromorphological thin section manufacture AMBI Lab, Universidad de La Laguna, Tenerife, Spain
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"du7v2","text":"Protocol for the manufacture of micromorphological thin sections performed at AMBI Lab, Universidad de La Laguna, Tenerife, Spain.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Oven drying of intact sediment blocks at 60 °C for 48:00:00

Impregnating of sample with a 7:3:0.1 ratio of a mixture of of polyester resin (Palatal strained resin UN1866, TNK composites), styrene (Styrene monomer (CAS: 100-42-5) UN2055, TNK composites) and a catalyzer (Methyl-ethyl-ketone (Luperox, CAS: 78-93-3), TNK composites)
After hardening, cutting of the blocks into 1 cm-thick slabs using a Euro-Shatal M31100 radial saw
Glueing onto 9 cm x 6 cm glass slides
Trimming to 1mm thickness using a Uniprec ATA Brilliant-220 precision cutting machine
Grinding to 30 µm thickness using a G&N MPS-RC-Geology grinding machine
creator: Lucia Leierer
